elements.sent-2411-001: The production of heavy quarks, which occurs during initial hard-scattering processes of hadronic collisions, can be described using perturbative Quantum Chromodynamics (pQCD) calculations.
elements.sent-2411-002: In heavy-ion collisions, heavy-quark production is sensitive to the entire evolution of the QGP.
elements.sent-2411-003: Investigating the thermalization of heavy quarks within the QGP provides valuable insights into its physical properties.
elements.sent-2411-004: By measuring open heavy-flavor hadrons and quarkonia, one can determine whether heavy quarks inherit flow from the QGP.
elements.sent-2411-005: Heavy quarks are also produced in smaller systems, where notable similarities have been observed at high $p_{\mathrm{T }$ between high-multiplicity p--Pb and Pb--Pb collisions for observables typically associated with QGP signatures, such as long-range azimuthal correlations.}
elements.sent-2411-006: These findings suggest the presence of collective phenomena in small systems ~\cite{AngularCorrelations .}
elements.sent-2411-007: Thus, investigating flow observables in the heavy-flavor sector is crucial for establishing and characterizing the collective behavior of charm and beauty quarks in small systems.
elements.sent-2411-008: This can be done using azimuthal correlations of emitted particles, through the measurement of the elliptic flow ,\( v_2 \), in various colliding systems such as pp, p--Pb, and Pb--Pb.
elements.sent-2411-009: In this contribution, we present the latest flow measurements of D mesons, of inclusive muons from hadron decays, and of quarkonia at different collision energies.
elements.sent-2411-010: We will also compare those results with theorical models.
elements.sent-2411-011: The ALICE apparatus measures inclusive charmonia at forward rapidity ($2.5 < y < 4$) through the dimuon decay channel.
elements.sent-2411-012: At midrapidity, prompt and non-prompt heavy-flavor hadrons (with non-prompt originating from b-hadron decays) can be well distinguished.
elements.sent-2411-013: The key detectors at midrapidity include the Inner Tracking System (ITS), which is essential for tracking, vertexing, and measuring charged-particle multiplicity, and the Time Projection Chamber (TPC), which is utilized for tracking and particle identification by measuring specific energy loss.
elements.sent-2411-014: At forward rapidity, the muon spectrometer, consisting of a system of absorbers, a dipole magnet, and tracking and triggering stations, is used for reconstructing and identifying muon tracks.
elements.sent-2411-015: The V0 detector, which ensures a minimum-bias trigger, measures particle multiplicity, centrality and event-plane angle for the flow measurements.
elements.sent-2411-016: Additionally, inclusive hadrons at both forward and midrapidity can be measured down to zero $p_{\mathrm{T }$.}
elements.sent-2411-017: A detailed description of the ALICE detector is available in Ref. ~\cite{Alice .}
elements.sent-2411-018: The following section presents the $v_2$ measurements of heavy-flavor hadrons across various collision systems.
elements.sent-2411-019: These results provide new constraints on theoretical models in order to understand heavy-quark collectivity.
elements.sent-2411-020: Open heavy-flavor hadrons, composed of light and heavy quarks, are crucial for studying the transport coefficients of QGP.
elements.sent-2411-021: In the left panel of Fig. 1, the average $v_2$ of prompt D\textsuperscript{0 , D\textsuperscript{+ , and D\textsuperscript{*+ mesons is compared with the non-prompt D\textsuperscript{0 -meson $v_2$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: The non-prompt D\textsuperscript{0 -meson $v_2$ is lower than the prompt non-strange D mesons with 3.2$\sigma$ significance in $2 < p_{\text{T < 8~\text{GeV /c$, indicating different degrees of thermalization between charm and beauty quarks in QGP.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: The weighted mean of the non-prompt D\textsuperscript{0 -meson $v_2$ in the measured $p_{\text{T }$ range ($2 < p_{\text{T < 12~\text{GeV /c$) is 2.7$\sigma$ above zero ~\cite{DMESONS .}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-024: In the right panel of Fig. 1, the same non-prompt D\textsuperscript{0 -meson $v_2$ (blue) is compared with the $v_2$ of electrons from beauty-hadron decays (b$\rightarrow$ c $\rightarrow$ e)~\cite{bdecay .}}
elements.sent-2411-025: Both measurements are also compared with theoretical models.
elements.sent-2411-026: The Linearized Boltzmann with diffusion model (LIDO) \cite{LIDO1 provides reasonable descriptions for these measurements, implementing beauty-quark transport in a hydrodynamically expanding QGP, including collisional and radiative processes.}
elements.sent-2411-027: Beauty-quark hadronisation via coalescence is considered in addition to the fragmentation mechanism.
elements.sent-2411-028: The good agreement between the predictions for B-meson and non-prompt D\textsuperscript{0 -meson $v_2$ from LIDO indicates that the decay kinematics do not play a significant role in the beauty-hadron $v_2$ measurements.}
elements.figure-2411-001: \includegraphics[width=0.85\linewidth]{Picture1.png} \caption{ Left: \( v_2 \) of non-prompt \( D^0 \) mesons (blue) and prompt non-strange \( D \) mesons (red) as a function of \( p_{\mathrm{T}} \) \cite{DMESONS}. Right: \( v_2 \) of non-prompt \( D^0 \) mesons \cite{DMESONS} (blue) and electrons from beauty-hadron decays \cite{bdecay} (red) as a function of \( p_{\mathrm{T}} \). Both measurements are measured in the 30--50\% centrality class in Pb--Pb collisions and are compared with LIDO model predictions \cite{LIDO1}, \cite{LIDO2}.} \label{fig:example}
elements.sent-2411-029: These measurements will provide important constraints to model predictions, allowing to extract accurately the spatial diffusion coefficient of beauty quark in QGP.
elements.sent-2411-030: Another way to study experimentally the flow in the heavy-quark sector is by using inclusive muons from heavy-flavor hadron decays.
elements.sent-2411-031: Figure 2 presents the $p_{\mathrm{T }$-differential elliptic flow coefficient, $v^{\mu _{2}$, of inclusive muons, with non-flow effects subtracted in the 0--20\% multiplicity class for p--Pb collisions at $\sqrt{s_{\mathrm{NN = 8.16$ TeV.}}}}}
elements.figure-2411-002: \includegraphics[width=1.0\linewidth]{picture2g.png} \caption{Flow of inclusive muon $v_2^{\mu}$ as a function of $p_{\mathrm{T}}$ at forward rapidity in high-multiplicity p--Pb collisions at $\sqrt{s_{\mathrm{NN}}} = 8.16$ TeV.} \label{fig:example}
elements.sent-2411-032: This new measurement covers for the first time a wide transverse momentum range of $0.5 < p_{\mathrm{T < 10$ GeV/$c$ in the forward rapidity region.}}
elements.sent-2411-033: The data reveal an initial increase in $v^{\mu _{2}$ with rising $p_{\mathrm{T }$, reaching a peak value of approximately 0.07 at $p_{\mathrm{T \sim 2$ GeV/$c$, followed by a decrease as $p_{\mathrm{T }$ continues to increase.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-034: The region of particular interest for studying the flow in the heavy-quark sector is $p_{\mathrm{T > 2$ GeV/$c$, where muons predominantly originate from heavy-flavor hadron decays and surpass contributions from light-hadron decays (such as charged-pion and -kaon decays at low $p_{\mathrm{T }$).}}}
elements.sent-2411-035: The results are compared to a multi-phase transport (AMPT) \cite{AMPTmodel and color-glass condensate (CGC) \cite{CGCmodel models in the left and right panel, respectively.}}
elements.sent-2411-036: Both models describe the data within uncertainties.
elements.sent-2411-037: The measurements were performed using two-particle correlations $v^\mu_{2}\{2\mathrm{PC}\}$ \cite{InclusiveMuons .}
elements.sent-2411-038: Statistical uncertainties (represented by vertical bars) were estimated using the Fourier fit method, while systematic uncertainties are depicted by empty boxes.
elements.sent-2411-039: These results suggest that heavy quarks exhibit significant flow at mid-to-high $p_{\mathrm{T }$ in p--Pb collisions.}
elements.sent-2411-040: In order to study the contribution of charmonia in collective effects in hadronic collisions, the elliptic flow $v_{2}$ of J/$\psi$ was measured across various colliding systems.
elements.figure-2411-003: \includegraphics[width=0.69\linewidth]{3sys.png} \caption{The \( p_{\mathrm{T}} \) dependence of $v_2^{\mathrm{J}/\psi}$ in pp, p--Pb \cite{p--Pbjpsi}, and Pb--Pb \cite{Pb--Pbjpsi} collision systems.} \label{fig:example}
elements.sent-2411-041: In Fig. 3, the flow measurement in pp is presented and compared with p--Pb \cite{p--Pbjpsi and Pb--Pb \cite{Pb--Pbjpsi flow data.}}
elements.sent-2411-042: The $v_2$ measurement in high-multiplicity pp and p--Pb collisions is obtained using azimuthal correlations between inclusive dimuons and charged hadrons.
elements.sent-2411-043: The Pb--Pb measurement is obtained using the scalar product method (SP).
elements.sent-2411-044: For p--Pb collisions, the elliptic flow of J/$\psi$ exhibits similar values to those observed in Pb--Pb collisions when $p_{\mathrm{T > 4$ GeV/$c$, suggesting a shared mechanism for generating flow in both collision systems.}}
elements.sent-2411-045: Nonetheless, the TAMU transport model \cite{RegenerationCharmonia , which successfully describes $v_2$ in Pb--Pb collisions, interprets this observation as a result of energy loss.}
elements.sent-2411-046: At low $p_{\mathrm{T }$, the flow contribution is understood to result from the regeneration of J/$\psi$.}
elements.sent-2411-047: Alternatively, other theoretical frameworks based on initial-state models, like CGC, predict that correlations among final-state particles could produce flow-like effects \cite{collectivitySmall .}
elements.sent-2411-048: In pp collisions, the J/$\psi$ $v_2$ does not significantly deviate from zero as a function of $p_{\mathrm{T }$.}
elements.sent-2411-049: This finding holds true even when considering the $v_2$ integrated over $p_{\mathrm{T }$ in the range $1 \leq p_{\mathrm{T \leq 12$ GeV/$c$, where the deviation from zero is within $1\sigma$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-050: Therefore, within the current uncertainties, no collective behavior is observed for the J/$\psi$ in high-multiplicity pp collisions at the LHC.
elements.sent-2411-051: Finally, the significantly larger Run 3 dataset as compared to Run 1 and 2 will enable more precise flow measurements, providing unprecedented breakthrough for the understanding of the flow of heavy quarks in hadronic collisions.
elements.figure-2411-004: \includegraphics[width=0.99\linewidth]{Picture4.png} \caption{Left: Fit of the invariant mass distribution $m_{ee}$ at midrapidity in pp collisions at \( \sqrt{s}= 13.6 \, \text{TeV} \), showing the extracted inclusive $\mathrm{J}/\psi$ and $\psi(\mathrm{2S})$ signals. Right: Fits of $m_{\mu\mu}$, $v_2^{\mu\mu}\{2\}$ and $v_2^{\mu\mu}\{4\}$ distributions at forward-rapidity in Pb--Pb collisions at \( \sqrt{s_{\mathrm{NN}}}= 5.36 \, \text{TeV} \), where the inclusive $\mathrm{J}/\psi$, $v_2^{\mathrm{J}/\psi}\{2\}$ and $v_2^{\mathrm{J}/\psi}\{4\}$ are also extracted (fraction of the total Pb--Pb statistics). } \label{fig:run3data}
elements.sent-2411-052: The left panels of Fig. 4 show the signal extraction of inclusive $\mathrm{J /\psi$ and $\psi(\mathrm{2S )$ from the $m_{ee}$ distribution at midrapidity ($|y| < 0.9$) in pp collisions at \( \sqrt{s = 13.6 \, \text{TeV \), with tracks selected by $p_{\mathrm{T > 1$ GeV/c.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-053: An event-mixing technique was applied for unlike-sign di-electrons.
elements.sent-2411-054: In the right panels of Fig. 4, the signal extraction of inclusive $\mathrm{J /\psi$ from the $m_{\mu\mu}$ distribution at forward rapidity ($ 2.5 <y < 4$) in Pb--Pb collisions at \( \sqrt{s_{\mathrm{NN = 5.36 \, \text{TeV \) is presented on top.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-055: Additionally, the two bottom right panels show the multi-particle cumulant coefficients $v_2^{\mu\mu}\{2\}$ and $v_2^{\mu\mu}\{4\}$ as function of $m_{\mu\mu}$.
elements.sent-2411-056: We have shown that non-prompt \(D^0\) mesons exhibit a lower \(v_2\) compared to prompt \(D\) mesons in Pb--Pb collisions, indicating different degrees of thermalization between charm and beauty quarks within the QGP.
elements.sent-2411-057: In p--Pb collisions, a notable elliptic flow is observed for inclusive muons at $p_{\mathrm{T > 2$ GeV/$c$, highlighting the contribution of heavy quarks to collective effects.}}
elements.sent-2411-058: For \(\mathrm{J /\psi\) mesons, the elliptic flow in p--Pb collisions shows similarities to Pb--Pb collisions, suggesting a shared mechanism for generating flow.}
elements.sent-2411-059: However, in high-multiplicity pp collisions, the \(\mathrm{J /\psi\) \(v_2\) remains close to zero, indicating the absence of significant collective behavior in these smaller collision systems.}
elements.sent-2411-060: Finally, using Run 3 data, we present the first signal extraction of \(\mathrm{J /\psi\) at mid and forward rapidity in pp and Pb--Pb collisions, respectively.}
elements.sent-2411-061: At forward-rapidity, a new "multiparticle cumulant" method is shown for the first time to extract $v_2^{\mathrm{J /\psi}$ with improved precision, reducing non-flow effects.}
